# -*- sh -*-
# See http://rigaux.org/language-study/diagram.html for the format of this file
#claude: auto-generated by Claude Code (inspired from my other lineage.txt)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Networking stack / library lineage
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

#src: wikipedia articles on different programs
#src: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_protocol_suite

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (protocols as context)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*TCP/IP
  1974
  # Cerf and Kahn, RFC 675 (1974), split into TCP/IP in 1981

*UDP
  1980
  TCP/IP
  # RFC 768, David Reed

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Historical (UNIX implementations)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*BSD sockets
  1983
  TCP/IP
  SY:2
  # BSD 4.2, Bill Joy and Sam Leffler
  # the socket API that became the standard everywhere

*STREAMS
  1984
  SY:6
  # Dennis Ritchie, System V, alternative to BSD sockets
  # message-passing I/O framework

*4.4BSD TCP/IP
  1993
  BSD sockets
  SY:2
  # THE reference implementation, basis for FreeBSD/macOS networking
  # Van Jacobson's congestion control (1988) was the key improvement

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Linux
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Linux TCP/IP
  1994
  BSD sockets
  SY:2
  # originally NET-1 (Ross Biro), NET-2 (Fred van Kempen)
  # NET-3/NET-4 rewrite by Alan Cox
  # most deployed networking stack in the world
*Linux 2.2 networking
  1999
  # netfilter (iptables), improved routing
Linux 2.4 networking
  2001
  # connection tracking, NAT, bridge support
*Linux 2.6 networking
  2003
  # NAPI (interrupt mitigation), TCP segmentation offload
*Linux 3.6 networking
  2012
  # TCP Fast Open
*Linux 4.9 networking
  2016
  # BPF/XDP for high-performance packet processing
  # BBR congestion control (Google, model-based, replaced loss-based Reno/CUBIC)
*Linux 5.6 networking
  2020
  # WireGuard merged, MPTCP support

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Educational
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Xinu TCP/IP
  1987
  TCP/IP
  # Douglas Comer, "Internetworking with TCP/IP" Vol II
  # most-studied networking stack after BSD

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Embedded
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

lwIP
  2001
  TCP/IP
  # Adam Dunkels, lightweight IP stack for embedded
  # used in ESP32, FreeRTOS, many microcontrollers

uIP
  2001
  TCP/IP
  # Adam Dunkels, even smaller than lwIP, for 8-bit microcontrollers
  # used in Contiki OS

*MirageOS TCP/IP
  2013
  TCP/IP
  # OCaml, clean-room unikernel network stack
  # part of MirageOS, compiles apps into specialized OS images

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Academic / Research
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*x-kernel
  1991
  TCP/IP
  # Larry Peterson, University of Arizona
  # framework for composing network protocols, very influential in courses

*Click Modular Router
  2000
  # Eddie Kohler, MIT, software router from composable elements
  # hugely influential on software networking research

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plan9
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Plan 9 /net
  1993
  BSD sockets
  SY:1
  # everything is a file: /net/tcp, /net/dns, no sockets API
  # dial()/announce()/listen() instead of socket/bind/connect

*IL
  1993
  TCP/IP
  SY:1
  # Plan 9 lightweight reliable protocol, replaced by TCP later

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*NetWare IPX/SPX
  1983
  SY:6
  # Novell, dominant LAN protocol before TCP/IP won

KA9Q NOS
  1985
  TCP/IP
  # Phil Karn, TCP/IP for DOS and amateur radio
  # brought TCP/IP to personal computers

*MacTCP
  1988
  TCP/IP
  SY:6
  # Apple's first TCP/IP stack for Macintosh
Open Transport
  1995
  MacTCP
  SY:6
  # Apple, replaced MacTCP, supported AppleTalk and TCP/IP
macOS Network.framework
  2018
  SY:6
  # Apple, modern networking API replacing BSD sockets on Apple platforms
  # user-space TCP/IP stack, QUIC/HTTP3 built-in

*Windows TCP/IP
  1993
  BSD sockets
  SY:6
  # NT 3.1, originally BSD-derived, now hugely deployed
*Windows TCP/IP (Vista)
  2007
  SY:6
  # Next Generation TCP/IP stack, dual IPv4/IPv6, receive window auto-tuning
*Windows TCP/IP (Win10)
  2015
  SY:6
  # TCP Fast Open, LEDBAT congestion control

Trumpet Winsock
  1994
  TCP/IP
  # Peter Tattam, TCP/IP stack for Windows 3.1
  # how most people first got on the Internet on Windows

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (SSL/TLS libraries)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*OpenSSL
  1998
  SY:2
  # fork of SSLeay (Eric Young, 1995)

LibreSSL
  2014
  OpenSSL
  # OpenBSD fork after Heartbleed

*BoringSSL
  2014
  OpenSSL
  SY:6
  # Google fork of OpenSSL

GnuTLS
  2000
  # GNU project, alternative to OpenSSL

wolfSSL
  2006
  # embedded TLS library, formerly CyaSSL

mbedTLS
  2009
  SY:6
  # ARM (formerly PolarSSL), for embedded

s2n-tls
  2015
  OpenSSL
  # Amazon, simpler/safer TLS library

rustls
  2016
  OpenSSL
  # Rust TLS library, memory-safe, increasingly adopted (curl, etc.)

Quiche
  2018
  # Cloudflare, QUIC/HTTP3 implementation in Rust

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (event / async I/O)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*libpcap
  1994
  BSD sockets
  # Van Jacobson, packet capture library (underlies tcpdump)

*libevent
  2002
  BSD sockets
  # Niels Provos, event notification library
  # used by Tor, memcached

libev
  2007
  libevent
  # Marc Lehmann, lighter alternative to libevent

*libuv
  2011
  libev
  # Joyent, cross-platform async I/O, created for Node.js

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (HTTP / high-level)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*libcurl
  1998
  BSD sockets
  # Daniel Stenberg, URL transfer library
  # used everywhere, dozens of language bindings

libmicrohttpd
  2007
  # GNU, embeddable HTTP server library

libsoup
  2001
  # GNOME HTTP client/server library

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (DNS)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

c-ares
  2004
  # async DNS resolution library, used by curl, Node.js

getdns
  2014
  # modern DNS API library, DNSSEC-aware

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Open source (other)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*libssh
  2003
  # SSH client/server library

libssh2
  2004
  libssh
  # different project, non-copyleft

ZeroMQ
  2007
  BSD sockets
  # iMatix, high-level messaging library
  # socket-like API for message patterns (pub/sub, req/rep)

nanomsg
  2013
  ZeroMQ
  # Martin Sustrik (ZeroMQ co-creator), simpler successor

nng
  2017
  nanomsg
  # nanomsg next generation

*gRPC
  2015
  SY:6
  # Google, HTTP/2-based RPC framework, protobuf serialization

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Industry
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*Winsock
  1993
  BSD sockets
  SY:6
  # Windows Sockets API, BSD-compatible

NDIS
  1989
  SY:6
  # Network Driver Interface Specification, Microsoft/3Com

*Java NIO
  2002
  SY:6
  # non-blocking I/O for Java, channels and selectors

Netty
  2004
  Java NIO
  SY:6
  # async event-driven networking framework for Java

#--------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Recent
#--------------------------------------------------------------------------

*smoltcp
  2017
  TCP/IP
  # Rust, user-space TCP/IP stack for embedded

DPDK
  2010
  # Intel, Data Plane Development Kit, kernel-bypass networking

*XDP
  2016
  # eXpress Data Path, eBPF-based high-performance packet processing in Linux

*Tokio
  2016
  libuv
  # Rust async runtime, networking-focused
